---
name: CLD Exploration
about: Time-boxed investigation to generate hypotheses when the problem is not yet understood
title: "EXP-YYYY-NNN: "
labels: cld-exploration
---

<!--
  Exploration mode is for when you don't yet have enough understanding
  to write a well-formed hypothesis. The goal is to generate hypotheses,
  not to validate them. No INIT or HYP document is required upfront.

  Exploration must be time-boxed. If it has no end condition,
  it is not exploration — it is avoidance.
-->

## What Are We Trying to Understand?

_State the question, not the answer. "We don't know why X is happening" is a valid start._

## Why We Can't Hypothesize Yet

_What is missing that prevents writing a falsifiable claim right now?_

## Time Box

**Ends:** _(date or event — "after 3 user interviews", "by end of week")_

**"Enough to hypothesize" looks like:** _(what signal or observation would let us write a HYP-\*.md?)_

## Planned Observations / Activities

- [ ]
- [ ]

## Outputs

At the end of the time box, this issue must produce:

- [ ] At least one candidate `HYP-YYYY-NNN.md` with a falsification signal, OR
- [ ] A documented decision to drop this line of inquiry (with reasoning)

_If neither is produced, the exploration was not completed — it was abandoned. Name it correctly._
